title: Phthirusa paniculata
taxonomy: Phthirusa paniculata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Loranthaceae, commonly known as mistletoe. It is a hemiparasitic plant, which means it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing another plant. The Loranthaceae family comprises around 70 genera and 2,000 species of mistletoes, many of which are found in tropical and subtropical regions. Phthirusa paniculata is native to the Americas and is known to infect a variety of host plants. Its classification reflects its unique relationship with its host and its adaptations for parasitic growth.
